description_long: This object is a realistic crab with a broad, flattened carapace and two prominent claws, one of which is noticeably larger and more robust than the other. The carapace is mottled with shades of brown, gray, and hints of green, giving it a natural, camouflaged appearance. The crab has ten appendages in total, including eight slender walking legs and two large chelae (claws), with the legs displaying a segmented, spiny texture. Two long, thin antennae and a pair of eyes on stalks protrude from the front of the carapace. The underside is lighter in color, with visible segmentation and jointed limbs. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, typical of a crustacean exoskeleton.
description: A lifelike crab with a broad, mottled carapace, two large claws, and spiny segmented legs. Its eyes are on stalks and the surface is hard and textured.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's broad carapace, both claws (one larger), and eyes on stalks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height of the crab, the upward angle of the claws, and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the crab's back, the symmetry of the legs, and the lighter underside.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
